message:
release: cut v1.0.19

6 rounds of session-mined reliability + security fixes on top of v1.0.18:

- Shell-injection hardening: escape worker IDs splicing into SSH commands
  (rchd/telemetry.rs); defense-in-depth worker-ID validation at config load
  (rchd/reload.rs); basename-based hook-command matching (rch-common/hooks.rs)
- Concurrency correctness: JSONL persist paths now emit one write_all per
  line so concurrent O_APPEND writers can't interleave into corrupt streams
  (history.rs, self_test.rs, fleet/history.rs); reserve_slots now refuses
  drain/drained/disabled workers
- Process-leak prevention: kill_on_drop on every timeout-wrapped ssh/scp/
  hook spawn — hook.rs, fleet/ssh.rs, cancellation.rs, benchmark_scheduler.rs,
  commands/hook.rs — so `tokio::time::timeout` no longer leaks ssh processes
- Pipe-buffer deadlock: invoke_cargo_metadata drains stdout/stderr on
  dedicated threads; 2.5MB of metadata for this very workspace no longer
  sits in a full pipe buffer until the 30s timeout trips and falls back
  to local builds
- Panic hardening: UTF-8 boundary-safe display truncation helper, NaN-filter
  on heartbeat percent, saturating_sub for daemon started_at, zero-divisor
  clamps on Rolling batch_size and AllAtOnce parallelism, RAII temp-file/
  dir cleanup for atomic_write and worker benchmark, panic-free short_hash
  for manifest display, lone-quote-tolerant dotenv parser
- Correctness: find_latest_backup now sorts by recorded creation time
  instead of lexicographic filename (v1.0.10 is newer than v1.0.9);
  reload.rs result.removed only counts actually-removed workers (drained
  ones now live in the warnings list)

5806 / 5806 tests pass. clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings clean.
cargo fmt --check clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
